name: Salt Dill Pickles
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Dry Dill Stems and Flower
3 To 4 ea Small Hot Chillies (to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch the cucumbers by washing and briefly immersing them in boiling water, then refreshing them immediately with cold water.
2. Arrange the cucumbers in a crock or large glass jar, alternating with dill stems, garlic cloves, bay leaves, peppercorns, and hot chilies to taste.
3. Pour enough cold water into the container to cover the cucumbers completely.
4. Measure out salt: 1 tablespoon (30g/1oz) for every 2 cups (500ml/1 pint) of water. Dissolve the salt in the measured water.
5. Pour the saltwater over the cucumbers, ensuring they are submerged.
6. Place vine leaves on top, then cover the crock with a heavy plate or wooden lid and add a weight to keep the cucumbers submerged.
7. Allow the pickles to ferment at room temperature for up to 2 weeks, skimming as needed during fermentation.
8. Once fermentation stops and no more bubbles rise to the surface, transfer the pickles to a dark, cool place or refrigerate.
